The glycerite extract of Barosma betulina contains compounds such as borneol and bornyl acetate, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Barosma betulina glycerite is often used as a natural remedy for urinary tract issues and is also said to have antioxidant and antiseptic properties.

recipe: To make barosma betulina glycerite for medicinal use, start by obtaining high-quality barosma betulina root, which is the plant's rhizome and roots.  Chop the rhizome and roots into small pieces and mix them with a carrier oil, such as coconut oil or olive oil, at a ratio of 1:5, to create a tincture.  After the tincture has been prepared, mix it with vegetable glycerin in a ratio of 1:1, and store the glycerite in a cool, dark place to allow the flavors and medicinal properties to mature.
